reasoning: The 360-day average fatalities measure is 31.167. Conflict fatality counts are typically characterized by a right-skewed, heavily overdispersed (e.g., Negative Binomial) statistical distribution where the median is lower than the mean. Thus, in a stable environment without structural changes, a randomly selected 30-day period intrinsically has a <50% chance of exceeding the baseline average (likely between 35-45%). I assign 0.38 for the closest resolution date (Nov 2), for which the 30-day window heavily overlaps with the recent known baseline. As we move out to later resolution dates (Nov 25, 2025, and Jan 24, 2026), there is greater uncertainty around structural shifts—such as protests or violence potentially surrounding future elections organized by the interim government. In accordance with forecasting principles regarding horizons, this uncertainty pushes the probabilities progressively closer to 0.5 (I assign 0.42 and 0.46 respectively).
